body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
Closes #474

## Todo
- can a `lmul_sqrt` method be created for such types? [Not yet implemented- note also not necessary for the EKP algorithms]

## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->
- Introduce the new matrix storage options `SVDplusD` (to store a covariance compactly) and `DminusTall` (to store the inverse of `SVDplusD` compactly.
- On construction of `ObservationSeries` the `SVDplusD` object is correctly inverted, and inverse stored as a `DminusTall`
- correctness of the lmul function and scalability checked with unit tests
- Documentation! See [here](https://clima.github.io/EnsembleKalmanProcesses.jl/previews/PR473/observations/)
## Example

```
using EnsembleKalmanProcesses, LinearAlgebra
n=100                                      # dim of observation
Γ_diag = Diagonal( 0.01*collect(1.0:n))    # diagonal noise
Z = randn(n, 5)                            # array with observation as columns
Γ_low_rank = tsvd_cov_from_samples(Z)      # cov of observations

Γ_sum = SVDplusD(Γ_low_rank, Γ_diag)       # compactly store the `Γ_low_rank + Γ_diag`
Observation_sum = Observation(
     Dict(
            "samples" => y_obs_test,
            "covariances" => Γ_sum, # internally builds the inverse of `SVDplusD`
            "names" => "cov_as_svd_plus_diag",
        ),
)

# build some process with it
utkiobj_sum = EnsembleKalmanProcess(..., observation_sum, Inversion())
```

<!---
Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/
- followed the style guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/
- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

-->

----
- [ ] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: SVDplusD, for representing a covariance as a sum of SVD and Diagonal without building it

body: ## Purpose Closes #466 Closes #467 Closes #468 ## To-do ## Content - Added `TransformUnscented` inspired from our EKTI based on using the Woodbury identity to perform the update linear algebra in the ensemble space not the data space in `src/UnscentedTransformKalmanInversion.jl`(i.e. from EKTF (https://journals.ametsoc.org/view/journals/mwre/129/3/1520-0493_2001_129_0420_aswtet_2.0.co_2.xml) - NB largely it is a similar formulation, with the main difference being the need to build "weighted" perturbations from the ensemble, not just 1/sqrt(N-1) * difference. - Share many of the current `Unscented` functions, with `TransformUnscented` - UKI & UTKI implementation with update-group indexing - fixed `Unscented(prior)` initialization for 1-dim parameter prior - full suite of unit tests (verifying utki and uki produce similar values) - Added UTKI into the loss_minimization example below. ### Misc - added "kwargs..." into update_ensemble for all methods - fixed small index bug for etki indexing (also in utki) with update-groups - Removed a warning for too-small ensemble size that is inappropriate for unscented methods - Add utilities to safely multiply `lmul` and `lmul!` by the observation noise, it's inverse. ## Examples: Examples, check with UKI - on `examples/LossMinimization/loss_minimization_finite_vs_infinite_ekp.jl` using `impose_prior=true` Order: Left (smooth loss), UKI & UTKI, Right (noisy loss) UKI & UTKI Both unscented variants (UKI/UTKI) behave the same on smooth loss and noisy loss (NB `impose_prior=true` has slight bias towards prior compared with e.g. EKS or other prior-imposing (a.k.a `X-infinite` variants) - on `examples/Accelerators/ExpSinAccelerated/` **Left = UKI, Right UTKI** ## Examples: Scaling - from unit tests solving problems with increased dimension and adding in regular UKI for comparison: ``` ┌ Info: 6 iterations of experiment uki with 1000-dim observation took 0.7976522949999998 seconds. └ (avg update: 0.13294204916666663) ┌ Info: 6 iterations of experiment utki with 1000-dim observation took 0.005414058999999999 seconds. └ (avg update: 0.0009023431666666665) Info: 4 iterations of experiment uki with 10000-dim observation took 109.81469675299999 seconds. └ (avg update: 27.453674188249998) ┌ Info: 4 iterations of experiment utki with 10000-dim observation took 0.099600575 seconds. └ (avg update: 0.02490014375) [UKI not attempted] ┌ Info: 3 iterations of experiment utki with 100000-dim observation took 0.48207968700000003 seconds. └ (avg update: 0.16069322900000002) [UKI not attempted] ┌ Info: 2 iterations of experiment utki with 1000000-dim observation took 5.737153102 seconds. └ (avg update: 2.868576551) ``` ---- - [ ] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: New UTKI variant `TransformUnscented`
